meta.title: AWS-DEA-3: Lakehouse Pipelines, Governance, and Streaming Reliability

questions.stem: A team needs to capture application events at high throughput and allow multiple consumers to process the same ordered stream independently. Which service is the best fit?
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Streams is built for scalable streaming ingestion with ordered shards and multiple consumers. The others are not purpose-built for high-throughput replayable streams.

questions.stem: A company wants to ingest database changes from Amazon RDS into Amazon S3 with minimal custom code and continuous CDC. Which service should it use?
questions.explanation: AWS DMS supports ongoing replication and CDC from relational engines into targets such as Amazon S3. The other services move files or SaaS data rather than database log-based CDC.

questions.stem: A streaming pipeline must enrich records with a custom Lambda function and then deliver the results to Amazon S3 and Amazon Redshift with managed buffering and retry behavior. Which service is most appropriate?

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink

questions.explanation: Amazon Data Firehose is the managed delivery service that buffers, optionally transforms with Lambda, and writes to destinations such as S3 and Redshift.

questions.stem: An AWS Glue batch job should process only new files added since the previous successful run. Which feature directly supports this pattern?
questions.explanation: Glue job bookmarks track previously processed data so subsequent runs can continue incrementally instead of re-reading the full dataset.

questions.stem: A data engineer is designing a near-real-time analytics pipeline on AWS. Which TWO statements are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink is suitable for stateful stream processing.
Amazon Data Firehose is mainly designed as a managed delivery service, not a general-purpose stream processor.
AWS Glue crawlers are the preferred engine for sub-second event processing.
Amazon S3 event notifications provide ordered replayable streams for many independent consumers.

questions.explanation: Managed Service for Apache Flink handles stateful streaming computations, while Data Firehose focuses on managed buffering and delivery. Glue crawlers and S3 notifications do not provide replayable streaming semantics.

questions.stem: A Glue ETL job writes hundreds of thousands of tiny Parquet files to S3. What is the best way to reduce file counts during the write?
questions.explanation: Controlling Spark partitions before write changes the number of output files. Bookmarks and S3 versioning do not compact batch output.

questions.stem: Which AWS service provides a serverless SQL interface for querying data directly in Amazon S3?
questions.explanation: Athena is the serverless interactive query service for data stored in S3.

questions.stem: A team needs a managed Apache Kafka service for producers and consumers that already use the Kafka protocol and tooling. Which service should they choose?
questions.explanation: Amazon MSK is the managed Kafka offering. Kinesis has a different API and protocol.

questions.stem: Which TWO benefits does AWS Glue Schema Registry provide for streaming data? (Select TWO)

options.text: It helps producers and consumers agree on schema versions.
It can enforce schema compatibility rules.
It automatically encrypts all S3 buckets in an account.
It replaces IAM policies for stream access control.

questions.explanation: Schema Registry manages event schemas and compatibility. It does not replace encryption or IAM controls.

questions.stem: A pipeline must route and transform records from Kinesis Data Streams with SQL or Flink logic before writing results. Which service is designed for that requirement?

options.text: Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink

questions.explanation: Managed Service for Apache Flink is AWS’s managed engine for SQL/Flink processing on streaming data.

questions.stem: What is the main role of an AWS Glue crawler?

options.text: Run Spark transformations on streaming events
Discover schema and populate the Data Catalog

questions.explanation: Glue crawlers infer schema and update catalog metadata for data stores.

questions.stem: A team wants to orchestrate several Glue jobs with conditional branching and retries when upstream validation fails. Which service is best suited to coordinate that workflow?
questions.explanation: Step Functions supports workflow orchestration, retries, branching, and service integrations including Glue jobs.

questions.stem: An Athena table over S3 data is slow and scans too much data for date-filtered queries. Which design change usually helps most?
questions.explanation: Athena can prune partitions when predicates align with partition columns, reducing scanned data and cost.

questions.stem: A Redshift table is frequently joined on `customer_id`. Which table design choice most directly improves co-location for joins?

options.text: Set the sort key to `sale_date` only

questions.explanation: A Redshift distribution key determines how rows are distributed across nodes and can reduce data movement for joins.

questions.stem: Which storage class transition mechanism lets a team automatically move older S3 objects to lower-cost tiers?
questions.explanation: S3 Lifecycle rules automate transitions and expiration for objects based on age or filters.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about the AWS Glue Data Catalog are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: It can act as a central metadata repository for analytics services.
Athena can use catalog table definitions to query S3 data.
It stores all source data rows directly inside the catalog.
It is only available to Amazon EMR and no other AWS service.

questions.explanation: The Data Catalog stores metadata and is shared by services such as Athena and Glue. It does not store actual datasets.

questions.stem: A team needs an open table format on S3 that supports ACID transactions and hidden partitioning for analytics. Which AWS service capability aligns best?

options.text: Apache Iceberg tables in AWS analytics integrations

questions.explanation: AWS analytics services support Apache Iceberg tables for transactional lakehouse-style data management on S3.

questions.stem: A company wants to retain a historical raw zone and a curated analytics zone in S3 with different prefixes, lifecycle rules, and access policies. What is the strongest reason to separate them logically?

options.text: It is required because S3 buckets can only store one file format
It allows independent governance, retention, and access controls by zone
It prevents Athena from reading Parquet

questions.explanation: Logical zone separation supports different retention, security, and operational controls for raw and curated data.

questions.stem: Which service is generally the best fit when a team needs a managed Hadoop or Spark cluster with fine-grained control over open-source frameworks and instance configuration?
questions.explanation: Amazon EMR is the managed cluster platform for frameworks such as Spark and Hadoop when more control is required than serverless ETL services provide.

questions.stem: A data lake table in S3 is queried from multiple engines. Which TWO practices usually improve analytical efficiency? (Select TWO)

options.text: Store columnar data in formats such as Parquet
Use partition columns that align with common filters
Write every event to a separate tiny file

questions.explanation: Columnar formats and useful partitioning improve scan efficiency. Tiny files and missing metadata usually hurt performance and interoperability.

questions.stem: Which AWS service is a data warehouse rather than a serverless query service or object store?
questions.explanation: Amazon Redshift is the managed data warehouse service.

questions.stem: A team wants to query external S3 data from Amazon Redshift without fully loading it into local Redshift tables. Which feature supports this pattern?
questions.explanation: Redshift Spectrum enables Redshift SQL queries over external S3 data through external schemas.

questions.stem: Which AWS service is the primary place to publish metrics, alarms, and logs for many data services?
questions.explanation: CloudWatch is AWS’s central monitoring service for metrics, alarms, dashboards, and many service logs.

questions.stem: A data engineer wants failed records in a streaming application to be replayable and diagnosable without stopping the whole pipeline. Which design is most appropriate?

options.text: Send failures to a dead-letter queue or failure stream for later analysis
Disable all logging to reduce cost

questions.explanation: Dead-letter queues or side outputs preserve failed records so they can be inspected and replayed safely.

questions.stem: Why would a team enable CloudWatch alarms on Glue job failures and duration anomalies?

options.text: To detect operational issues quickly and trigger notifications or remediation

questions.explanation: Operational alarms improve visibility and allow faster response when jobs fail or run unexpectedly long.

questions.stem: Which TWO activities are common parts of cost-aware data operations on AWS? (Select TWO)

options.text: Choosing file formats and partitioning to reduce Athena scan volume
Right-sizing or auto-scaling compute where supported
Storing all monitoring data indefinitely without review

questions.explanation: Storage layout and right-sized compute reduce cost. Disabling encryption is not acceptable, and retaining all telemetry forever may increase spend.

questions.stem: A nightly workflow should not start downstream transformations until a validation query confirms that expected partitions arrived in S3. Which approach best matches this requirement?

options.text: Use Step Functions to gate later steps on the validation result

questions.explanation: Step Functions can coordinate validation, branching, and downstream execution based on prior results.

questions.stem: Which Glue feature helps developers build and visually author ETL jobs, but still runs jobs on the Glue service?
questions.explanation: Glue Studio is the visual authoring environment for Glue ETL and ELT pipelines.

questions.stem: A team needs to preserve infrastructure definitions for data pipelines so environments can be recreated consistently. Which practice is best?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as code improves reproducibility, reviewability, and consistent deployment of pipeline resources.

questions.stem: Which TWO characteristics make checkpointing important in stateful stream processing systems? (Select TWO)

options.text: It supports recovery from failures without recomputing from scratch in many cases.
It helps preserve progress and state consistently over time.
It removes the need for monitoring or alerting.
It guarantees that downstream schemas can never change.

questions.explanation: Checkpointing preserves progress and state for recovery. It does not replace observability or schema management.

questions.stem: A Glue job run unexpectedly failed overnight. Which source should an operator inspect first for service logs and metrics tied to the run?
questions.explanation: CloudWatch is the primary observability destination for Glue job logs, metrics, and alarms.

questions.stem: Which AWS service centralizes fine-grained permissions for data lake resources such as tables, columns, and locations?
questions.explanation: Lake Formation builds on IAM and the Glue Data Catalog to manage centralized data lake permissions.

questions.stem: A team wants analysts to query only a subset of rows in a shared table based on region. Which Lake Formation feature directly addresses this need?
questions.explanation: Lake Formation data filters support row and column-level authorization patterns for governed tables.

questions.stem: What is the best place to store database credentials used by a Glue job so the secrets can be rotated centrally?

options.text: In an S3 object with public access

questions.explanation: Secrets Manager is designed for secure secret storage and rotation.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about IAM are correct in AWS data platforms? (Select TWO)

options.text: IAM controls who can call AWS APIs and use resources.
IAM policies can be combined with service-specific permissions such as Lake Formation grants.
IAM is only relevant for EC2, not serverless analytics services.

questions.explanation: IAM is the foundation for AWS authorization and often works alongside service-level permissions such as Lake Formation.

questions.stem: A company stores sensitive data in S3 and wants to limit accidental exfiltration over the public internet from private workloads. Which control is most directly relevant?

options.text: Configure private network paths such as VPC endpoints where applicable

questions.explanation: Private connectivity patterns such as VPC endpoints reduce the need to traverse the public internet for service access.

questions.stem: Which encryption model is commonly used to protect data at rest in Amazon S3?
questions.explanation: S3 commonly uses server-side encryption, including AWS managed or customer managed KMS keys.

questions.stem: Why might a team use Lake Formation LF-tags?

options.text: To express tag-based access control across governed resources

questions.explanation: LF-tags enable tag-based authorization so access policies can scale across many resources.

questions.stem: Which capability lets a team audit who is allowed to access data resources through centrally managed AWS permissions and grants?

options.text: Governed permissions in Lake Formation alongside IAM

questions.explanation: Lake Formation and IAM together define and govern who can access data resources and how.

questions.stem: A data engineer shares an Athena-accessible dataset across teams. What is the strongest reason to keep the Glue catalog definitions accurate and well-governed?

options.text: The catalog is part of access, discovery, and consistent query semantics across tools
Athena can only read CSV files
Catalog accuracy disables the need for IAM
Catalog tables automatically fix bad data values

questions.explanation: Well-managed metadata improves discovery, interoperability, and governance across analytics services.
